title: Workflow Commander — Card List + Execution Panel Component
description: Build the Workflow Commander frontend component: a card-based workflow list that fetches available workflows from GET /api/workflows, and an execution panel that shows step-by-step progress when a workflow is triggered via POST /api/workflows/execute. Reads active workflow state from the activeWorkflows store and receives real-time updates via WebSocket.
action: Create src/lib/client/components/WorkflowCommander.svelte with two sub-views: (1) Workflow card list fetched from /api/workflows, each card shows workflow name/category/description with 'Run' button, (2) Execution panel showing step progress for active workflow from activeWorkflows store. On 'Run' click, POST to /api/workflows/execute with workflowId and params. Real-time step updates come from activeWorkflows store (fed by WebSocket). Replace the placeholder content area in +page.svelte with this component.

criteria: Component script section contains fetch("/api/workflows") or fetch("api/workflows")
Each workflow rendered as a card with name, category, description, and 'Run' button
Execution panel shows step-by-step progress for active workflow from activeWorkflows store
Step status indicators match: pending=yellow, running=blue with spinner, complete=green check, failed=red X
Component uses translatedName ?? name for all user-visible labels
Component uses (translatedName ?? name) for all user-visible labels — template contains translatedName
grep -E " (delegate|session|skill) " src/lib/client/components/WorkflowCommander.svelte returns 0 matches in user-visible string literals (not import paths or comments)
convergence.verification: npx vite build && manual visual check in browser
convergence.definition_of_done: Workflow Commander displays available workflows as cards, triggers execution via API, and shows real-time step progress from WebSocket updates

files.change: Create Svelte 5 component with workflow card list + execution panel. Fetches /api/workflows on mount. POSTs /api/workflows/execute on Run click. Reads activeWorkflows store for real-time step progress. Two views: list view (cards) and execution view (step progress).

implementation: Create src/lib/client/components/WorkflowCommander.svelte. Script section: import activeWorkflows, displayMode, connectionState from '$lib/client/stores/index.js'. Import type WorkflowExecution, WorkflowMeta from '$lib/shared/types.js'.

Define state: let workflows = $state<WorkflowMeta[]>([]), let loading = $state(true), let error = $state<string|null>(null), let activeView = $state<'list'|'execution'>('list'), let currentExecutionId = $state<string|null>(null).

Template - list view: grid of workflow cards. Each card: div with class 'workflow-card', shows workflow.translatedName??workflow.name (use translate helper), category badge, description, 'Run' button (disabled if !connected).

Template - execution view: header with workflow name + back button. Step list: iterate currentExecution.steps. Each step: status indicator (icon based on step.status), step.translatedName??step.name, output preview if available. Progress bar based on completed/total steps.
Status icon helper: stepStatusIcon(status:StepStatus): string. 'pending'->'○', 'running'->'◉' (with CSS animation pulse), 'complete'->'✓', 'failed'->'✗', 'skipped'->'—'.

Styling: workflow-card: background #313244, border-radius 8px, padding 16px, margin-bottom 12px. Run button: background #cba6f7, color #1e1e2e, border-radius 6px. Step list: similar to tree but flat, 40px row height. Progress bar: height 4px, background #45475a, fill #a6e3a1 for complete, #89b4fa for running.
Note: +page.svelte wiring is handled by TASK-004

success_metrics: Component renders in browser, fetches workflows, and shows execution progress

reference.pattern: Svelte 5 runes + store subscription from +page.svelte and stores/index.ts. Fetch pattern for API calls. Color scheme from existing CSS.

rationale.chosen_approach: Two-view design (list + execution) keeps the UI simple for MVP. Card-based list is familiar and scannable. Execution panel with step progress provides the real-time feedback required by SC-2. Direct fetch() calls for API routes keep the data flow transparent.
decision_factors: SC-2 requires real-time step progress updates
Card layout is standard for workflow/action selection
activeWorkflows store already receives WebSocket updates
rationale.tradeoffs: No workflow parameter form in MVP — params are empty {}. Parameter configuration can be added in Phase 2 when workflow schemas are better understood.

risks.risk: /api/workflows returns empty list if maestro CLI not available
risks.mitigation: Show empty state with message 'No workflows available. Ensure maestro CLI is installed and a project is open.'

risks.risk: WebSocket updates may lag behind execution start
risks.mitigation: Execution panel shows 'Starting...' state immediately after POST, then transitions to step progress when WebSocket updates arrive
